title: Pawia mexicana
taxonomy: Pawia mexicana is a species of sea hare that belongs to the family Aplustridae, which is a group of large sea hares found in tropical and subtropical waters. Pawia mexicana is classified as a type of sea slug, specifically an opisthobranch. Within the opisthobranch group, Pawia mexicana is classified as a member of the order Nudibranchia, which includes slugs with exposed gills. The specific classification of Pawia mexicana also includes the class Gastropoda, which comprises all types of snails and slugs. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between Pawia mexicana and other related species.
